old_claim.note: Digital Signal Processing (DSP) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: ADSL transceivers depend on modem signal-processing techniques, including DMT line coding and integrated signal-processing silicon, to carry broadband data over impaired copper loops.

source_shape.source_locator: IEEE ETHW milestone describes the DMT prototype, ANSI selection of DMT as ADSL line code, innovative signal processing, and integrated signal-processing silicon.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: ADSL broadband over copper depended on specific modem line coding and signal-processing implementation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports retyping DSP from generic enabler to required because the scoped ADSL transceiver technology cannot perform the high-rate copper-loop modulation without signal-processing techniques.

ontology_before: The graph treated DSP as generic enabling context for DSL.
ontology_after: The graph treats DSP as a required method layer for standardized ADSL transceivers.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: digital_subscriber_line_dsl -> digital_signal_processing is required and source-backed.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened to any low-speed digital signaling on subscriber loops.
A more specific DMT modem line-code node replaced the broad DSP prerequisite.
short_reason: ADSL transceivers require signal-processing line coding.
